title: 5 Redeeming Certificate-style Canada Savings Bonds
body_text:
5	Redeeming Certificate-style Canada Savings Bonds
5.1	Introduction
The Government of Canada annually issues two types of certificate-style Canada Premium Bonds: Regular Interest âRâ Canada Savings Bonds and Compound Interest âCâ Canada Savings Bonds.
Each certificate consists of an upper part and a lower part. The upper part or Bond Head is removed by the redemption agent and has no value. The lower part or Redemption Certificate is processed for redemption. When a certificate-style Canada Savings Bond is redeemed, both the Bond Head and Redemption Certificate should be presented for redemption.
5.2	Determining the Redemption Rate
Annually in October, the interest rate applicable to all outstanding issues of Canada Savings Bonds is prescribed by Order in Council. Immediately thereafter, the Bank of Canada updates their Online Tool used to determine redemption values, which provide a month-by-month breakdown of redemption values through to the following October.
When referring to the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Officeâs online tool or the Redemption Value Tables (S40), proceed as follows:
1.
Ensure youâre using the correct certificate or serial number.
2.
Ensure youâre using the correct series âRâ or âCâ.
3.
If redeeming more than one bond of the same (or different) value(s), calculate each bond individually. Do not multiply or divide redemption values of other denominations.
Important
:	The redemption values for any Canada Savings Bonds from CSBs Series S1-S31 and CSBs Series S32-S45 can only be obtained from the Redemption Value Table (S40).
5.3	Processing Redemptions on Certificate-style Bonds
To redeem a certificate-style Canada Savings Bond, proceed as follows:
1.
Ensure the entire bond is presented for redemption (i.e., both the Bond Head and the Redemption Certificate).
2.
Obtain the amount to be paid from the Bank of Canada Unclaimed Properties Officeâs Online Tool or the Redemption Value Tale (S40). Record the amount in the space on the face of the Redemption Certificate.
3.
Have the bondholder sign the reverse of the Redemption Certificate. If the bondholder is not a member, obtain identification. Verify the signature and record identification on the bond.
4.
Remove the Bond Head and any perforated edges on the Redemption Certificate. Retain the Bond Head until a deposit acknowledgement is received from Central 1.
5.
Stamp the reverse of the Redemption Certificate with the credit unionâs Endorsement Stamp. Do not stamp the face of the bond. The stamp must include the redemption date and the name and branch, or transit number, of your financial instruction. The stamp acts as a guarantee that the signature on the bond is the registered ownerâs.
6.
If required, prepare a tax statement for the bondholder. Refer to the Redemption Value Tables (S40) for the tax statement applicable to the series being redeemed.
Note:	If a tax form is not specified, the Bank of Canada will issue a T5 information slip directly to the Registered Owner. See Section 4.8, Preparing Tax Statements, for preparation and distribution of tax forms.
5.4	Settlement and Adjustments for Certificate-style Canada Savings Bonds
Central 1 will settle daily for redeemed bonds through the credit unionâs Central 1 Canadian Dollar current account. Central 1 settles for the amount in the redemption value box.
If the incorrect amount is written in the redemption value box, the credit union will not be credited immediately for the correct amount. The Bank of Canada will pick up the error and process the required adjustment, but this may take up to a month. The minimum amount for adjustments must be greater than $6.99. The Bank of Canada will not
process adjustments on amounts of $6.99 or less.
5.5	Redeeming a Bond with One Part Missing
Depending on which part of the bond is missing, the bond may or may not be eligible for redemption.
Bond Head Missing
If the Bond Head is missing, process the Redemption Certificate as outlined in Section 5.3, Processing Redemptions on Certificate-style Bonds. If there is doubt as to the authenticity of the certificate or the validity of the registration, send the Redemption Certificate on collection. See Section 4.7, Handling Suspect, Fraudulent, or Mutilated Bonds or Coupons.
Redemption Certificate Missing
If the Redemption Certificate is missing, the bond has no value. Advise the bondholder to report the missing Redemption Certificate to the Bank of Canada at the following address:
Bank of Canada
234 Wellington Street
Ottawa, Ontario K1A 0G9
END
